name: Publication Certification
description: Validates that the Publish Artifact Orchestrator has run all domain audits (including design slop audit) and produced a GO or CONDITIONAL certification. NO-GO blocks publication.

any_of.description: Publication certification report with GO/CONDITIONAL/NO-GO status

validations.description: Publication certification or approval report must exist
Publication certification must not have FAIL status in frontmatter
Publication certification must not be NO-GO
Frontmatter status must not be FAIL (parsed from YAML)
PUBLICATION GATE: Client component must import SOURCES and IMAGE_CREDITS — bibliography and image attribution must be present in the shipped code
PUBLICATION GATE: Client component must reference at least 5 images from the IMAGES constant — photorealistic essays require substantial photography
PUBLICATION GATE: images.ts must contain R2-hosted URLs (images.esy.com) — hotlinking external URLs is prohibited
PUBLICATION GATE: images.ts must not contain hotlinked Wikimedia URLs — all images must be self-hosted on R2
PUBLICATION GATE: Spot-check that R2 image URLs are reachable (HTTP 2xx) — final defense against broken images shipping to production

notes: G8 is the FINAL automated quality gate before human publication approval (G9). It validates both the audit report AND the actual source code. Critical lesson: audit documents can pass while the implementation is broken (e.g., 404 image URLs, missing bibliography section) when the same agent writes both code and audits. G8 now cross-checks the client component directly for SOURCES, IMAGE_CREDITS, and minimum image count from the IMAGES constant. It enforces that images.ts contains only R2-hosted URLs (images.esy.com) — hotlinking external URLs is prohibited since G4.7 migrates all images to R2. These source-code validations cannot be fooled by a self-certifying audit document.
